blocks.text: Viceroy Vincent the Vice

As a businessman, an Oasis is great news for me. I immediately saw all of the possibilities: casinos, hotels, brothels. But these crazy Kitties who treacherously attacked us are a matter of war, and I'm not going to fight. There are tokens made for battles, and there are tokens made for a cup of hot tea: gentlemen.
Lee

It's sad to admit, but we're all going to die in this fucking desert. It seemed clear to everyone that the Oasis would save us, but I did not expect anything good from it. An Oasis in the desert is always a trap. And it turned out to be exactly what I thought it was. ETHdamn Kitties have long taken a liking to this place and were waiting for the wayfarers, exhausted and hopeless. There is nothing more boring than killing mutated roboKitties for a few servings of gas, but I could not help it.
The Eye

Good day and a great fight! My buddies were attacked by the Kitties, but all I can do is protect the weak and those who pay me. Anyways, to me, there is no difference between protectng and killing. Alas, I could not save some of my friends. We will bury them as heroes. I'm not a master of making speeches, but among us there are those who can glorify the exploits of our fallen brothers.
Roosevelt

“Live fast, die young” sounds cool, especially when it does not concern you personally. Our soldiers fought great, but some can't have been that great, because they are now somehow dead. It's a pity, though, no one asked them to die. Okay, I'll go get myself some gas. By the way, roboKitties are super tasty. You should try them. All right, I'm off. See ya. Ah, I almost forgot. Go fuck yourselves.
Corley

Oh, how I hate CryptoKitties. I have spent almost all of my ammo on them. I'm so angry that I would have shot them again, but I'm not gonna waste more bullets on their shitty bodies. Also, the gunpowder in their meat can be an unpleasant surprise. I'll ask Daedalus to make more bullets for me, but this weirdo might refuse, and I don’t wanna mess around him.
Mrs. Stacey

It's terribly difficult to walk on sand in these shoes. I remember the good old days when my fans carried me aloft in their hands… Well, a real Star should save face, even during the Apocalypse. It’s nice here, good that we followed Temoto. Selfie-time! I hope there are SPA-salons there in this promised or whatever-it’s-called land. I definitely need a facial. Whaaaaat?! What the fu... oh... the heck is going on here?! It is a trap!
Tracey

I remember when we were young, the Great War was already being fought in every corner of our world. We were children of war, we saw nothing but death around us. Harold and I did not even have time to be just a brother and sister. What are we doing wrong? I hate this freaking world!
Ashley Mazel
Today was sad day.
I used to have a few CryptoKitties back when I was a child. It was heartbreaking to see my old friends get slaughtered like that.
Oh and some tokens from our group died, so I guess that was kind of sad too.
If I’m being honest though, I’m glad the Thing exploded. Whatever it was, it creeped me the fuck out.

Unrelated to the battle, but relevant to the day: I watched Zergaldo try to chat up Layla. She told him it wouldn’t happen if he was the last token on the planet. Maybe her and I would get along after all.
How to Campfire Roast CryptoKitties in the desert
Ingredients:
1 CryptoKitty
1 bottle of gas
Black pepper
Sea salt
Make a fire, then make a skewer.
Freshen, skin and eviscerate the CryptoKitty.
Pour a generous portion of gas on the carcass and sprinkle with freshly ground black pepper and salt. Let it marinade for a while.
Hang the carcass over the fire. Do not forget to turn the CryptoKitty over from time to time, so it cooks evenly.
A few minutes before the meat is ready, add a little gas for flavor.
If you are hungry, the meat will be ready whenever you want. But the average cooking time is 30 minutes.
